capabilities.name: Staged sprint lifecycle + operator QA platform journeys
sharedInvariants: served frontend bundle equals dist/web bundle
exactly one verified managed LandOS server owns port 3141
operator data survives browser refresh and managed restart
QA infrastructure issues only read-only requests against operator data
browserAssertions: workspace shell renders visible LandOS content in a real browser
deal-cards API reconciles with the visible page
content persists across reload and npm run landos:restart
knownLimitations: fixture selection for property-state journeys is heuristic; journeys report fixture_unavailable when no safe match exists
mutating intake journeys require explicit --allow-mutations plus a QA fixture address and remain manual-gated

capabilities.name: Acquisitions Lead Workspace (canonical composed operator view)
regressionFixtures: existing verified lead with disputed acreage (acceptance example: deal 19)
existing unresolved lead with recorded resolution attempt (deal 20)
sharedInvariants: The workspace renders only the versioned /api/landos/lead-workspace/:id read model; WS1-WS3 conclusions are consumed, never recomputed client-side.
Canonical identity.acreageBasis outranks the legacy per-report reconciliation wherever both exist; a disputed basis never renders as reconciled.
Exactly five approved strategies: Cash Flip; Novation or Double Close; Subdivide or Minor Split; Land-Home Package; Improvement Then Flip.

A genuine requested-vs-resolved parcel conflict is a hard stop: both identifiers and the block reason are disclosed; no property intelligence, valuation, offer range, strategies, or seller brief may present as actionable.
Previously accepted operator identity records never change from an implicit intake match; explicit targeting plus operator confirmation is required.
A screening run whose gate is off contributes zero facts to the operator record.
Unknown values render as unavailable, never invented; resolution provenance is preserved and pre-verification snapshots are labeled historical.
The legacy Deal Card is quarantined from the new flow: deal-card-root never renders in the workspace path, while legacy /landos?deal= deep links stay compatible.
browserAssertions: lead-workspace-strategy count is exactly 5 on desktop and at 412x915
deal-card-root absent in the workspace flow, including after a Deal Library row click
Deal Library row click lands on the workspace and records the ?deal= deep link; refresh returns to the workspace
genuine-apn-conflict journey: conflict disclosed with both identifiers and block reason; conflicted parcel never presented as verified
API/UI reconciliation between the workspace payload and visible values
knownLimitations: Comps table shows top 6 sold and top 6 active validated rows with an honest showing line; the full validated registry is not paginated in the UI.
Deal 19's restored resolution snapshot lacks the original lane-level detail (unrecoverable; disclosed in its activity log).
Legacy Deal Card comp $/acre rendering (strong-comp-market) and the thin-comp-market fixture remain owned by paused sprint-2026-07-15-dealcard-v2 ws4-comps-valuation.
Tyler final usefulness review is pending; this freeze is regression protection only, not Tyler sign-off.
